name: NAD+ repletion improved aged mouse stem-cell function
summary: NAD+ repletion is a cross-hallmark animal signal for aged stem-cell function, with overlap into mitochondrial dysfunction.
statement: In aged mice, NAD+ repletion with nicotinamide riboside improved mitochondrial and stem-cell function across aged stem-cell compartments and enhanced lifespan.
population_or_model: Aged mice and tissue stem-cell compartments
time_horizon: Preclinical intervention period with lifespan and tissue stem-cell assays
quantitative_note: The staged baseline uses this as animal support for stem-cell resilience, not as direct human stem-cell rejuvenation evidence.
caveats: This evidence overlaps strongly with the NAD and redox restoration track.
Human NAD precursor studies do not yet show comparable stem-cell rejuvenation endpoints.
Mouse lifespan and stem-cell readouts may not translate to human regenerative benefit.
